---
title: "Pickerelweed (Pontederia cordata)"
slug: "pontederia-cordata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622713102/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Pickerelweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Commelinales"
tax_family: "Pontederiaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Commelinales", "Pontederiaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622713102/medium.jpg" alt="Pickerelweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/hoodpooh">
hoodpooh</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342001100">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Commelinales
-   **Family:** Pontederiaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=52599)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in shallow freshwater habitats.

## Natural history notes

An emergent aquatic plant characterized by its broad, heart-shaped leaves and spikes of bright blue to violet flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in shallow freshwater habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and slow-moving streams. It grows during the warmer months and provides important habitat and nectar resources for aquatic insects and pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/52599-Pontederia-cordata)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/993)
